introduction: This spec defines the requirements for initializing the `web/` frontend scaffold for af-hub, covering toolchain setup (Vite, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, shadcn/ui), dev proxy configuration, Makefile build targets, a single Hello World route, and frontend documentation. Note: requirements referencing external library APIs (shadcn/ui, TanStack Query, React Router, Vite) are based on unverified PRD assumptions and should be cross-checked against installed package versions before implementation.

glossary./api/*: URL pattern for all API requests that are proxied to the Go backend.
glossary./healthz: Health probe endpoint proxied to the Go backend.
glossary./readyz: Readiness probe endpoint proxied to the Go backend.
glossary.QueryClient: The TanStack Query client instance that must be wrapped around the React component tree.
glossary.QueryClientProvider: The TanStack Query React context provider that makes the QueryClient available to all child components.
glossary.React Router: Client-side routing library configured in this spec with a single route at `/`.
glossary.TanStack Query: React Query v5 library used for async state management; a QueryClient provider wrapper is configured in this spec with no active queries.
glossary.Vite: The build tool and dev server used for the frontend project.
glossary.af-hub: The name of the application whose frontend scaffold is being initialized.
glossary.docs/web-ui.md: The frontend development guide documenting prerequisites, setup, dev server, build process, project structure, and component conventions.
glossary.make web-build: Makefile target that runs the Vite production build by running `npm run build` inside `web/`.
glossary.make web-dev: Makefile target that starts the Vite dev server with hot reload by running `npm run dev` inside `web/`.
glossary.node_modules/: The directory where npm installs project dependencies; its absence triggers auto-install in Makefile targets.
glossary.npx shadcn-ui@latest init: The CLI command used to copy shadcn/ui component source files into the project tree.
glossary.package.json: npm manifest file for the `web/` project, listing scripts and dependencies.
glossary.shadcn/ui: A component library whose source files are copied directly into the project tree via `npx shadcn-ui@latest init`, rather than installed as an npm dependency.
glossary.tsconfig.json: TypeScript compiler configuration file for the `web/` project.
glossary.vite.config.ts: The Vite configuration file where the dev proxy rules are defined.
glossary.web/: The frontend project directory at the repo root, containing its own package.json and all frontend source code.
glossary.web/dist/: The output directory for the Vite production build, containing compiled static assets.
glossary.http://localhost:8080: Default Go backend server URL for dev proxy target.
glossary.http://localhost:8080/healthz: Proxied liveness probe URL via the Go backend.
glossary.http://localhost:8080/readyz: Proxied readiness probe URL via the Go backend.
glossary.npm run dev: NPM script that starts the Vite dev server with hot reload.
glossary.npm install: NPM command to install project dependencies.
glossary.npm run build: NPM script that runs the Vite production build.
glossary.npm run lint: NPM script that runs ESLint and TypeScript type checking.
glossary.npm run preview: NPM script that previews the production build locally.
glossary./: Root route path for the Hello World page.
glossary./unknown: An undefined route path used to test fallback behavior.
glossary./api/: API path prefix proxied to the Go backend.

user_story.goal: have a cleanly initialized `web/` project with its own package.json and tsconfig.json at the repo root
user_story.benefit: I can work on the frontend independently from the Go backend without tooling conflicts

acceptance_criteria.action: resides at the repository root in a directory named `web/`, containing its own `package.json` and `tsconfig.json` with no shared configuration files with the Go backend
uses Vite as the build tool and dev server, React with TypeScript as the UI framework, Tailwind CSS for styling, React Router for client-side routing, and TanStack Query for async state management
copies shadcn/ui component source files into the project tree via `npx shadcn-ui@latest init`, not as an npm installed dependency
requires Node.js 18 or later, as required by Vite 5+
wraps the React application component tree so all components have access to the TanStack Query QueryClient

edge_cases.action: the setup documentation in `docs/web-ui.md` specifies the minimum Node.js version requirement so the developer can upgrade before running any commands
edge_cases.return_contract: Setup fails with a clear npm or Vite error message indicating the Node.js version is incompatible
edge_cases.error_condition: Node.js version installed is older than 18

user_story.goal: have the Vite dev server automatically proxy /api, /healthz, and /readyz requests to the Go backend
user_story.benefit: I can develop the frontend against the real backend without CORS issues or manual request rewiring

acceptance_criteria.action: proxies the request to `http://localhost:8080`, preserving the path and query string
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response from the Go backend is returned to the browser unchanged
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a request matching `/api/*` is received during development

acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response from the Go backend is returned to the browser unchanged
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a request to `/healthz` is received during development

acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Response from the Go backend is returned to the browser unchanged
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a request to `/readyz` is received during development

acceptance_criteria.action: defines proxy rules for `/api/*`, `/healthz`, and `/readyz` targeting port 8080 to match the Go server's default configuration

edge_cases.action: returns a proxy error response to the browser indicating the upstream connection failed, without crashing the Vite dev server
edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 502 or connection-refused error visible in the browser's network tab; the dev server continues running
edge_cases.error_condition: the Go backend at `http://localhost:8080` is not running when a proxied request is made

user_story.goal: run `make web-dev` and `make web-build` from the repo root
user_story.benefit: I can start the dev server or build the frontend using the same make interface as the rest of the project

acceptance_criteria.action: checks for the presence of `node_modules/` in `web/`; if absent, runs `npm install` in `web/` first, then runs `npm run dev` in `web/` to start the Vite dev server with hot reload
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Vite dev server starts and outputs its listening URL to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: checks for the presence of `node_modules/` in `web/`; if absent, runs `npm install` in `web/` first, then runs `npm run build` in `web/` to compile the production build into `web/dist/`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Build succeeds and compiled static assets are written to `web/dist/`; exit code 0 on success, non-zero on failure

edge_cases.action: the Makefile target propagates the non-zero exit code so CI pipelines and callers can detect the failure
edge_cases.return_contract: make exits with the same non-zero code as npm; error output from npm is visible on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: `npm run build` exits with a non-zero code due to TypeScript or bundling errors

edge_cases.action: the Makefile target propagates the non-zero exit code and does not proceed to the dev or build step
edge_cases.return_contract: make exits with a non-zero code; npm error output is visible on stderr
edge_cases.error_condition: `npm install` fails (e.g., network error, registry unavailable)

user_story.goal: have standard npm scripts for dev, build, lint, and preview
user_story.benefit: I can use familiar npm tooling without memorizing project-specific commands

acceptance_criteria.action: starts the Vite dev server with hot module replacement enabled
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Dev server starts and prints the local URL to stdout

acceptance_criteria.action: runs the Vite production build, emitting compiled static assets to `web/dist/`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0 on success with assets written to `web/dist/`; non-zero exit code on failure

acceptance_criteria.action: runs ESLint across all TypeScript source files and performs TypeScript type checking via tsc --noEmit
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Exit code 0 if no lint or type errors; non-zero exit code with error details printed to stderr otherwise

acceptance_criteria.action: starts a local static server serving the `web/dist/` output to preview the production build
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Preview server starts and prints the local URL to stdout

edge_cases.action: prints all errors with file names and line numbers and exits with a non-zero code
edge_cases.return_contract: Non-zero exit code; errors listed on stderr or stdout
edge_cases.error_condition: TypeScript type errors or ESLint violations exist in the source

user_story.goal: navigate to `/` in a browser and see a minimal placeholder page
user_story.benefit: I can verify the routing and rendering pipeline is working end-to-end before implementing real pages

acceptance_criteria.action: renders a page displaying the text `af-hub` and a brief placeholder message with no navigation, sidebar, or complex layout components
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: Page renders with visible text containing 'af-hub' and a placeholder message; HTTP 200 from the dev server

acceptance_criteria.action: is configured with a single route mapping `/` to the Hello World page component, serving as the routing foundation for future page additions

edge_cases.action: renders a minimal not-found indicator or falls back to the root route, without crashing the application
edge_cases.return_contract: Browser displays a non-blank page; no JavaScript console errors from unhandled route failures
edge_cases.trigger: a browser navigates to any path other than `/` (e.g. `/unknown`)

user_story.goal: find a clear guide at `docs/web-ui.md` covering prerequisites, setup, dev server, build, project structure, and component conventions
user_story.benefit: I can get up and running without asking teammates for basic setup instructions

acceptance_criteria.action: documents Node.js version prerequisites (Node 18+), npm install setup, how to run the dev server (`npm run dev`), how to run the production build (`npm run build`), the project directory structure under `web/`, and shadcn/ui component conventions including how components are copied into the tree
includes a link to `docs/web-ui.md` and mentions the `make web-dev` and `make web-build` targets so developers can discover the frontend workflow from the project root

correctness_properties.title: Proxy rules cover all required backend endpoints
correctness_properties.for_any: request path that starts with `/api/`, equals `/healthz`, or equals `/readyz`
correctness_properties.invariant: the Vite dev server must have a matching proxy rule in `vite.config.ts` forwarding the request to `http://localhost:8080`

correctness_properties.title: Makefile targets always install dependencies before running
correctness_properties.for_any: invocation of `make web-dev` or `make web-build` where `node_modules/` is absent in `web/`
correctness_properties.invariant: `npm install` is run in `web/` before any Vite command is executed, ensuring the build never fails due to missing packages

correctness_properties.title: npm scripts exit codes are propagated
correctness_properties.for_any: npm script (`npm run dev`, `npm run build`, `npm run lint`) that exits with a non-zero code
correctness_properties.invariant: the non-zero exit code is visible to the calling process (Makefile or CI runner), enabling failure detection

correctness_properties.title: Hello World route always renders without crashes
correctness_properties.for_any: browser navigation to `/` with the React app loaded
correctness_properties.invariant: the page must render the text `af-hub` and a placeholder message with no uncaught JavaScript exceptions in the browser console

correctness_properties.title: QueryClientProvider wraps the entire component tree
correctness_properties.for_any: React component rendered within the application
correctness_properties.invariant: the component is a descendant of QueryClientProvider, ensuring TanStack Query hooks are available without additional setup in future specs

execution_paths.title: Developer starts frontend dev server from repo root

steps.action: Runs `make web-dev` from the repository root
Detects that `node_modules/` is absent in `web/` and runs `npm install` in `web/`
Starts with hot module replacement enabled, configures proxy rules for `/api/*`, `/healthz`, and `/readyz` targeting `http://localhost:8080`, and prints the local URL to stdout
Opens the printed URL in a browser and sees the Hello World page rendering 'af-hub' and the placeholder message

execution_paths.title: Developer runs production build from repo root

steps.action: Runs `make web-build` from the repository root
Checks for `node_modules/` in `web/`; if absent, runs `npm install` in `web/`
Compiles TypeScript, bundles React components, and emits static assets to `web/dist/`
Exits with code 0, signalling success to CI or the calling shell

execution_paths.title: Frontend dev proxy forwards API request to Go backend

steps.action: Issues a browser request to `http://localhost:5173/api/some-endpoint` while the Vite dev server is running
Matches the `/api/*` proxy rule defined in `vite.config.ts` and forwards the request to `http://localhost:8080/api/some-endpoint`
Receives the proxied request, processes it, and returns an HTTP response
Returns the Go backend's response to the browser unchanged
Receives the API response with no CORS errors, as the proxy presents a same-origin response

error_handling.condition: Node.js version is older than 18 when running `npm install` or Vite commands
error_handling.behavior: npm or Vite exits with an error message indicating incompatibility; `docs/web-ui.md` documents the Node 18+ prerequisite so developers can upgrade

error_handling.condition: Go backend at `http://localhost:8080` is not running when a proxied request arrives at the Vite dev server
error_handling.behavior: Vite dev server returns a 502 or connection-refused error to the browser without crashing; the dev server remains running and ready to serve subsequent requests

error_handling.condition: `npm run build` exits with a non-zero code due to TypeScript or bundling errors
error_handling.behavior: The `make web-build` Makefile target propagates the non-zero exit code; error output from npm/Vite is visible on stderr; CI pipelines detect the failure

error_handling.condition: `npm install` fails (e.g., network error or registry unavailable) during a `make web-dev` or `make web-build` invocation
error_handling.behavior: The Makefile target propagates npm's non-zero exit code and does not proceed to run the dev server or build step; the error is visible on stderr

error_handling.condition: TypeScript type errors or ESLint violations exist when `npm run lint` is executed
error_handling.behavior: All errors are printed with file names and line numbers; the command exits with a non-zero code so CI pipelines can detect lint failures

error_handling.condition: Browser navigates to a path other than `/` that has no matching React Router route
error_handling.behavior: Application renders a minimal not-found indicator or falls back gracefully without throwing unhandled JavaScript errors or crashing the React tree
